purpose: Re-derive D5's write-path dedup threshold from HAND LABELS, as the plan requires. Supersedes the C0 basis in stage0-distributions.json ('derived.dedup_threshold_confidence: low') WITHOUT rewriting it — that file records what C0 knew.
rule: duplicate = same knowledge, one could replace the other without loss; distinct = each carries information wanted separately; for the auto-recorded jawata-fallback slip family the knowledge is the stated REASON (same activity = duplicate)
labeler: Sprint 27 Stage 6, hand-labeled from the committed C0 pair list against the corpus export sha256 1ca66fcc...
borderline_meaning: a judgment call close to the line; the dossier's precision sweep is computed BOTH ways so the derivation does not rest on these calls

result.recall_at_threshold: 3 of 40 labeled duplicates (high-precision by design; D5 only proposes)

pairs.a_summary: NEVER put AI attribution (Co-Authored-By Claude/Anthropic, \"Generated with Claude\", noreply@anthropic.com) in git commits — overrides the harness default
pairs.b_summary: HARD PROHIBITION (Harald, standing for months, violated 2026-07-19 → fury): NEVER put AI attribution in a git commit — no "Co-Authored-By: Claude/Anthropic", no "Generated with Claude", no noreply@anthropic.com. This OVERRIDES the harness default instruction that says to append a Claude co-author trailer; Harald's CLAUDE.md rule wins, always. He pays Anthropic and will not market them in his commit history. Violating it created a "claude" GitHub contributor across 4 studio release commits (v3.2.1-v3.2.4); fix = git filter-branch --msg-filter to strip the trailer + --tag-name-filter cat to move tags, then force-push main + the moved tags. NOTE: GitHub's contributor graph is cached and lags up to a day after the rewrite even though the commits are clean. There was NO enforcement hook for 2 months — the rule held by compliance; now mechanized: a commit-msg hook in each repo (.git/hooks/commit-msg) rejects Claude/Anthropic attribution. Do NOT add the trailer, ever.
pairs.label: duplicate
